summary: Tested Yousuf Hakim portfolio: 5/5 backend pytest cases pass (root, valid contact create+persist, invalid email 422, missing field 422, list sorted desc with no _id). Frontend: theme toggle, 4 portfolio cards w/ hover opacity change, Process 3 steps (Strategy/Edit & Motion/Final Polish + 01/02/03), Pricing 'Custom Pricing', FAQ accordion expands, Navbar Let's Talk -> /contact, Hero CTA -> /contact, Contact form submit -> success toast, smooth scroll to #portfolio/#process/#pricing/#faq all PASS.

minor.issue: Inside the except handler, `logger.exception(...)` is called but `logger` is defined AFTER `app.include_router(api_router)` (line 116). If any internal failure ever triggers the except path, it will raise NameError instead of returning HTTP 500. Move the `logging.basicConfig` and `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` block to the top of the module (right after imports).

action_items: Optional: move `logger` initialisation to top of /app/backend/server.py so the contact POST exception handler doesn't NameError on internal failures.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logger used before defined (line 89 vs 116). Move logger init above routes.
server.py: contact POST returns 200 (FastAPI default) instead of 201; not required but consider status_code=201 for create semantics.
Hero headline 'Turning raw clips into mini masterpieces.' is split into per-word spans so text selectors must use partial matches (visual rendering verified correct).
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Test contact submissions prefixed with TEST_ via POST /api/contact during pytest run; safe and discoverable for cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend uses uuid string ids, datetime stored as ISO string. Contact POST returns 200 (not 201) - tests accept both. Hero headline text is split per-word so use partial selectors. Theme toggle writes class on <html> ('lenis dark' -> 'lenis light') and data-theme on .App root. Portfolio uses cdn.coverr.co videos which may not load (don't assert network playback).
